stemHtml: An aircraft is executing an approach to land when the tower controller instructs the pilots to initiate a go-around, since the runway is occupied by another aircraft. The pilots immediately initiate the go-around. The pilot flying is applying maximum power and pitching up the aircraft, while the pilot monitoring programms the go-around path on the FMS and waits for the pilot flying to request the required configuration changes. The tower controller then informs the pilots that the go-around is no longer necessary. What is the most probable outcome?

options.a: The pilot flying will take over the task of monitoring the flight instruments, while the pilot monitoring concentrates on the radio call.
options.b: Both pilots will briefly discuss whether the go-around can still be aborted and a landing can be made.
options.c: Neither pilot will consciously receive the radio message, because both of them will be too focused on performing the flight manoeuvre.
options.d: The pilot monitoring will be distracted by the radio call, his attention would be drawn away from monitoring the instruments.
